title: AWS launches carbon-aware cloud zones for greener workloads
summary: A new placement option helps teams reduce compute emissions during peak demand windows.
content: Cloud providers are expanding sustainability controls for infrastructure teams. The new policy-based placement option lets teams prioritize regions and windows that have lower carbon intensity. This helps product teams keep performance while reducing climate impact for non-latency-sensitive jobs.
author: Editorial Desk

title: Kubernetes security checklist for startups shipping fast
summary: Five practical controls that close common security gaps in small engineering teams.
content: Startups often move quickly and defer hardening until later. This guide covers baseline controls: strict RBAC, secrets management, image scanning, network policies, and runtime alerts. These controls give strong protection without slowing down developer velocity.
author: Ops Team

title: Serverless cost traps you should measure weekly
summary: A monitoring rhythm to avoid runaway bills from retries, logs, and idle integrations.
content: Serverless can cut operating overhead, but costs can spike from retry storms, noisy logs, and unbounded event sources. Teams should build a weekly review around top functions by spend, error rates, and cold-start behavior. Visibility is the fastest path to savings.
author: Cloud Finance Lab
